channel.title: QiOne
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Complete Estate Planning Document Package
item.description: This collection of estate planning templates and worksheets is provided by QiAlly as a document preparation and organizational aid — not as legal representation or professional legal advice.Our goal is to help you understand, organize, and prepare your essential estate documents in clear, modern language. You retain full control and responsibility for how these documents are completed, executed, and filed.QiAlly and its affiliates, contractors, and contributors are not attorneys and do not provide legal services or legal advice. The materials provided here are general templates intended for educational and organizational purposes only. You are not establishing an attorney–client relationship with QiAlly, its representatives, or any of its platforms. You are responsible for reviewing and, if necessary, having these documents evaluated by a licensed attorney in your state or jurisdiction. You understand that laws vary by state, and certain documents may require specific language, signatures, or notarization to be legally valid where you reside. You accept full responsibility for ensuring that your final documents comply with all applicable laws and accurately reflect your personal wishes. QiAlly LegalEase provides: Professional document organization and formatting support. Standardized, legally-inspired templates for personal adaptation. Guidance on how to structure, store, and reference your personal and estate records. AI-assisted drafting tools for clarity and consistency. QiAlly LegalEase does not: Offer legal representation or appear in court. File legal documents on your behalf. Guarantee legal outcomes or enforceability. Provide state-specific legal advice. Review each document carefully and customize it for your personal situation. Consult a licensed attorney to confirm compliance with your state's estate and health-care laws. Sign and notarize where required. Store copies safely — one at home, one with your designated agents, and one in your secure QiVault or drive. This folder contains all core estate planning and end-of-life directive templates for [Full Legal Name]. Each document may be customized and notarized separately to ensure validity under state law.Documents Included: Last Will and Testament Living Will and Health Care Power Durable Power of Attorney (Financial) HIPAA Authorization Revocable Living Trust Beneficiary Designations Final Wishes Addendum ⚖️ Note: These templates are general legal drafts. Always have them reviewed and notarized per your state's requirements. Your Last Will and Testament is your final set of instructions about how your estate, belongings, and responsibilities should be handled after your death. It ensures your wishes are respected and prevents the default state laws from deciding who inherits what.A Will: Names an Executor to handle your estate. Lists Beneficiaries who will receive your property. Designates Guardians for minor children or dependents. May outline funeral or memorial preferences. Your Will speaks after you're gone — it's your final voice in how things are handled. Paired with your Living Will and Powers of Attorney, you'll have full coverage both during and after your lifetime. ⚖️ Disclaimer: This overview is provided by QiAlly Document Services for educational and document preparation purposes only. It is not legal advice. You should have all documents reviewed by a licensed attorney in your jurisdiction. I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], of [City, State], being of sound mind, declare this to be my Last Will and Testament.I revoke all prior wills and codicils.I appoint [Cody Rice-Velasquez] as my Executor (Personal Representative).If he cannot serve, I appoint [Alternate Representative].I give, devise, and bequeath all my tangible and intangible property to Cody Rice-Velasquez, my child, including all real estate, vehicles, jewelry, bank accounts, and personal effects.If he does not survive me, I designate [Alternate Beneficiary].If any minor dependents exist, I nominate Cody Rice-Velasquez as guardian of their person and estate.All debts and taxes shall be paid from the estate before distribution.Signature of Testator: ___ Date: ___Witness #1: ___ Witness #2: ___Notary Public (if required): ___Your Living Will and Medical Power of Attorney (POA) — also called Advance Health Care Directives — protect your right to decide what happens to your body and medical care when you can't speak for yourself.These documents: Record your wishes for end-of-life care (life support, resuscitation, feeding tubes, pain relief, etc.). Appoint a Health Care Agent to make medical decisions for you. Provide legal access to your medical information (often paired with a HIPAA release). Having these directives in place relieves your family of uncertainty and ensures your voice is heard even in silence. It's one of the most compassionate things you can do for those who love you. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Created by QiAlly Document Services for document preparation and informational use. This content is not a substitute for legal counsel. Verify state requirements and have your documents properly witnessed and notarized. I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], being of sound mind and over the age of eighteen (18), make this declaration to direct my family, physician, and all concerned as to my wishes for medical care, end-of-life decisions, and distribution of my property in the event I become unable to communicate my decisions.Address: [Full Address] Date of Birth: [MM/DD/YYYY]I hereby designate [Full Legal Name of Son/Daughter: Cody Rice-Velasquez], of [Address, City, State, Zip], as my Primary Health Care Agent and Durable Power of Attorney for Health Care.If my Primary Agent is unable or unwilling to serve, I designate: Alternate Agent: [Full Legal Name] Address: [Address] Relationship: [Relationship to Declarant] My Agent shall have full authority to make all health care decisions on my behalf, including but not limited to: Consent to or refusal of any medical treatment, procedure, medication, or surgery. Access to my medical records. Admission to or discharge from hospitals, nursing homes, or hospice care. Authorization of organ donation or autopsy. End-of-life decisions, including withdrawal of life-support, resuscitation (DNR/DNI), and comfort care. This Power of Attorney becomes effective immediately and remains valid even if I become incapacitated.If I have an incurable or irreversible condition and my attending physician has determined that my death is imminent, or if I am in a persistent vegetative state with no reasonable expectation of recovery, I direct that: Life-sustaining treatment (including CPR, mechanical ventilation, or tube feeding) shall not be administered if it only prolongs the dying process. I wish to receive comfort care and pain management even if it may hasten death. I do / do not wish to donate my organs and tissues. (Circle one and specify wishes here: ____) I appoint [Cody Rice-Velasquez] as my Attorney-in-Fact for all financial and property matters.This includes the authority to: Access and manage all bank accounts, investments, and property. Pay bills, taxes, and debts. Buy, sell, or transfer property in my name. Apply for benefits (Social Security, Medicare, etc.). File and manage legal or tax documents. This authority shall be durable and remain in effect in the event of my incapacity.Upon my death, I direct that all my personal and real property, including but not limited to household goods, vehicles, jewelry, and financial accounts, shall pass to:Primary Beneficiary: [Cody Rice-Velasquez], my child.If the above beneficiary does not survive me, I designate: Alternate Beneficiary: [Name, Relationship, Address]If guardianship or conservatorship becomes necessary, I nominate [Cody Rice-Velasquez] to serve in that role.Declarant Signature: ___ Name: [Full Legal Name of Mother] Date: [Date]Witness #1: Signature: ___ Printed Name: ____ Address: _ Date: ____Witness #2: Signature: ___ Printed Name: ____ Address: _ Date: ____STATE OF __ COUNTY OF ____Subscribed and sworn before me on this _ day of ____, 20____, by [Full Legal Name of Mother], who is personally known to me or has produced valid identification.Notary Public: ___ My Commission Expires: __ Seal: ☐A Durable Power of Attorney (POA) allows you to appoint someone you trust — called your Attorney-in-Fact — to handle your financial and legal matters if you are ever unable to do so yourself.The Financial POA authorizes your agent to: Pay bills, file taxes, and manage bank accounts. Access investments and property. Apply for benefits or handle insurance. Conduct business transactions on your behalf. This document stays valid even if you become incapacitated — that's why it's durable. Your chosen agent should be financially responsible, organized, and aligned with your best interests. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Provided by QiAlly Document Services for general educational use. This is not legal advice. Laws vary by state — please consult a licensed attorney before relying on or filing this document. I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], designate Cody Rice-Velasquez as my Attorney-in-Fact, with full authority to handle all financial and legal matters on my behalf.My Agent shall have authority to: Access and manage bank accounts, investments, and retirement funds. Pay bills, file taxes, and manage property. Buy, sell, lease, or transfer property. Handle insurance, benefits, and claims. Engage attorneys, accountants, or financial advisors as needed. This Power of Attorney is durable, remaining effective even if I become incapacitated.Effective Date: Immediately upon signing. Governing Law: State of [Your State].Signature: ___ Date: ___Witnesses: Notary: ___(See Document 2 introduction above)I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], authorize the release of my protected health information (PHI) to the following:Primary Authorized Recipient: Cody Rice-Velasquez Address: [Address] Relationship: Son / Health Care ProxyI understand this authorization allows my providers to: Disclose medical history, treatment, and billing information. Discuss my condition and care with the named individual(s). This authorization remains valid until revoked in writing or until my death.Signature: ___ Date: ___Witness: ___A Revocable Living Trust is a legal arrangement that lets you transfer ownership of assets into a private trust during your lifetime, while still maintaining control of them. After your death, those assets can pass directly to your chosen beneficiaries — skipping probate entirely.A Trust helps: Avoid probate, keeping your affairs private. Ensure smooth transitions of property or accounts. Provide flexibility, as it can be amended or revoked while you're alive. Appoint a Successor Trustee to manage everything if you're incapacitated. While your Will covers what's left in your name, your Trust can own your property, accounts, and valuables — making transitions faster, cleaner, and often less expensive for your heirs. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Prepared by QiAlly Document Services for document preparation use only. This explainer is not legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney to ensure compliance with your state's trust and estate laws. This Revocable Living Trust is made on [Date] by [Mother's Full Legal Name], as Grantor and Trustee.Grantor transfers into the Trust all property listed in Schedule A.Upon the death of the Grantor, all trust assets shall be distributed to Cody Rice-Velasquez.If the Grantor is unable to act, Cody Rice-Velasquez shall serve as Successor Trustee with full powers to administer, manage, and distribute assets.This trust may be revoked or amended by the Grantor at any time during her lifetime.Signature of Grantor: ___ Date: ___Witnesses: ___ Notary: ___Beneficiary Designations determine who inherits certain financial accounts automatically upon your death — often bypassing probate entirely. These are typically set up directly with your bank, retirement plan, or insurance provider.This document helps you: Track all your beneficiary designations in one place. Ensure consistency between your forms and your Will or Trust. Prevent conflicts or outdated listings (e.g., ex-spouses or deceased relatives). Beneficiary forms override your Will — if they don't match, the financial institution follows the designation, not the Will. Regularly review and update these after major life changes. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Prepared by QiAlly Document Services. Provided for organizational and recordkeeping purposes only. This summary does not constitute legal advice. Always confirm your designations directly with each institution. This document lists designated beneficiaries for accounts that pass outside the will.Reviewed and Updated: [Date]The Final Wishes Addendum captures your personal, emotional, and spiritual preferences — the human side of your legacy. It guides loved ones in understanding how you want to be remembered and what matters most to you.Use this section to record: Burial, cremation, or donation preferences. Desired music, readings, or service themes. Messages, memories, or legacy notes for family and friends. Digital asset or social media account instructions. While not always legally binding, this document provides heartfelt guidance during difficult moments. It's your voice of love, clarity, and remembrance — ensuring your story ends with intention and grace. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Authored by QiAlly Document Services. This explainer and its templates are for informational purposes only and should not be treated as legal or professional advice. I wish to be: Buried Cremated Donated for medical research Preferred Funeral Home: [Name] Desired Location/Plot: [Description]Preferred songs, readings, or themes: "__" To my loved ones: "__" My digital accounts, passwords, and photos are stored at: [Location / Cloud Vault / Drive folder]Signature: ___ Date: ___At QiAlly™, we believe legal clarity shouldn't require a law degree. These templates are meant to empower you to take ownership of your legacy, protect your family, and document your wishes with confidence — all while keeping it smart, simple, and legally aware. "LegalEase™ – because your peace of mind shouldn't come with fine print." Prepared by: QiAlly Document Services For educational and organizational use only.Document Package Version: Complete Estate Planning Collection Last Updated: [Date] QiDecimal ID: 3.10.00
item.dc:creator: QiAlly LegalEase

item.title: Introduction: Final Wishes & Personal Addendum
item.description: The Final Wishes Addendum captures your personal, emotional, and spiritual preferences — the human side of your legacy. It guides loved ones in understanding how you want to be remembered and what matters most to you.Use this section to record: Burial, cremation, or donation preferences. Desired music, readings, or service themes. Messages, memories, or legacy notes for family and friends. Digital asset or social media account instructions. While not always legally binding, this document provides heartfelt guidance during difficult moments. It’s your voice of love, clarity, and remembrance — ensuring your story ends with intention and grace. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Authored by QiAlly Document Services. This explainer and its templates are for informational purposes only and should not be treated as legal or professional advice.
item.dc:creator: QiAlly Document Services

item.title: Introduction: Beneficiary Designations
item.description: Beneficiary Designations determine who inherits certain financial accounts automatically upon your death — often bypassing probate entirely. These are typically set up directly with your bank, retirement plan, or insurance provider.This document helps you: Track all your beneficiary designations in one place. Ensure consistency between your forms and your Will or Trust. Prevent conflicts or outdated listings (e.g., ex-spouses or deceased relatives). Beneficiary forms override your Will — if they don’t match, the financial institution follows the designation, not the Will. Regularly review and update these after major life changes. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Prepared by QiAlly Document Services. Provided for organizational and recordkeeping purposes only. This summary does not constitute legal advice. Always confirm your designations directly with each institution.
item.dc:creator: QiAlly Document Services

item.title: Introduction: Revocable Living Trust
item.description: A Revocable Living Trust is a legal arrangement that lets you transfer ownership of assets into a private trust during your lifetime, while still maintaining control of them. After your death, those assets can pass directly to your chosen beneficiaries — skipping probate entirely.A Trust helps: Avoid probate, keeping your affairs private. Ensure smooth transitions of property or accounts. Provide flexibility, as it can be amended or revoked while you’re alive. Appoint a Successor Trustee to manage everything if you’re incapacitated. While your Will covers what’s left in your name, your Trust can own your property, accounts, and valuables — making transitions faster, cleaner, and often less expensive for your heirs. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Prepared by QiAlly Document Services for document preparation use only. This explainer is not legal advice. Consult a qualified attorney to ensure compliance with your state’s trust and estate laws.
item.dc:creator: QiAlly Document Services

item.title: Introduction: Living Will and Medical Power of Attorney
item.description: Your Living Will and Medical Power of Attorney (POA) — also called Advance Health Care Directives — protect your right to decide what happens to your body and medical care when you can’t speak for yourself.These documents: Record your wishes for end-of-life care (life support, resuscitation, feeding tubes, pain relief, etc.). Appoint a Health Care Agent to make medical decisions for you. Provide legal access to your medical information (often paired with a HIPAA release). Having these directives in place relieves your family of uncertainty and ensures your voice is heard even in silence. It’s one of the most compassionate things you can do for those who love you. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Created by QiAlly Document Services for document preparation and informational use. This content is not a substitute for legal counsel. Verify state requirements and have your documents properly witnessed and notarized.
item.dc:creator: QiAlly Document Services

item.title: Introduction: Durable Power of Attorney for Finances
item.description: A Durable Power of Attorney (POA) allows you to appoint someone you trust — called your Attorney-in-Fact — to handle your financial and legal matters if you are ever unable to do so yourself.The Financial POA authorizes your agent to: Pay bills, file taxes, and manage bank accounts. Access investments and property. Apply for benefits or handle insurance. Conduct business transactions on your behalf. This document stays valid even if you become incapacitated — that’s why it’s durable. Your chosen agent should be financially responsible, organized, and aligned with your best interests. ⚖️ Disclaimer: Provided by QiAlly Document Services for general educational use. This is not legal advice. Laws vary by state — please consult a licensed attorney before relying on or filing this document.
item.dc:creator: QiAlly Document Services

item.title: Introduction: Last Will and Testament
item.description: Your Last Will and Testament is your final set of instructions about how your estate, belongings, and responsibilities should be handled after your death. It ensures your wishes are respected and prevents the default state laws from deciding who inherits what.A Will: Names an Executor to handle your estate. Lists Beneficiaries who will receive your property. Designates Guardians for minor children or dependents. May outline funeral or memorial preferences. Your Will speaks after you’re gone — it’s your final voice in how things are handled. Paired with your Living Will and Powers of Attorney, you’ll have full coverage both during and after your lifetime. ⚖️ Disclaimer: This overview is provided by QiAlly Document Services for educational and document preparation purposes only. It is not legal advice. You should have all documents reviewed by a licensed attorney in your jurisdiction.
item.dc:creator: QiAlly Document Services

item.title: Estate Planning Document Prep – Introduction & Disclaimer
item.description: This collection of estate planning templates and worksheets is provided by QiAlly* as a document preparation and organizational aid** — not as legal representation or professional legal advice.Our goal is to help you understand, organize, and prepare your essential estate documents in clear, modern language. You retain full control and responsibility for how these documents are completed, executed, and filed.QiAlly and its affiliates, contractors, and contributors are not attorneys and do not provide legal services or legal advice. The materials provided here are general templates intended for educational and organizational purposes only. You are not establishing an attorney–client relationship with QiAlly, its representatives, or any of its platforms. You are responsible for reviewing and, if necessary, having these documents evaluated by a licensed attorney in your state or jurisdiction. You understand that laws vary by state, and certain documents may require specific language, signatures, or notarization to be legally valid where you reside. You accept full responsibility for ensuring that your final documents comply with all applicable laws and accurately reflect your personal wishes. QiAlly LegalEase provides: Professional document organization and formatting support. Standardized, legally-inspired templates for personal adaptation. Guidance on how to structure, store, and reference your personal and estate records. AI-assisted drafting tools for clarity and consistency. QiAlly LegalEase does not: Offer legal representation or appear in court. File legal documents on your behalf. Guarantee legal outcomes or enforceability. Provide state-specific legal advice. Review each document carefully and customize it for your personal situation. Consult a licensed attorney to confirm compliance with your state’s estate and health-care laws. Sign and notarize where required. Store copies safely — one at home, one with your designated agents, and one in your secure QiVault or drive. At QiAlly™, we believe legal clarity shouldn’t require a law degree. These templates are meant to empower you to take ownership of your legacy, protect your family, and document your wishes with confidence — all while keeping it smart, simple, and legally aware. “LegalEase™ – because your peace of mind shouldn’t come with fine print.” Prepared by: QiAlly Document Services For educational and organizational use only.
item.dc:creator: QiAlly LegalEase

item.title: Estate Planning Index
item.description:
This folder contains all core estate planning and end-of-life directive templates for [Full Legal Name]. Each document may be customized and notarized separately to ensure validity under state law.Documents Included: .01_Last_Will_and_Testament
.02_Living_Will_and_Health_Care_Power
.03_Durable_Power_of_Attorney_Financial
.04_HIPAA_Authorization
.05_Revocable_Living_Trust
.06_Beneficiary_Designations
.07_Final_Wishes_Addendum ⚖️ Note: These templates are general legal drafts. Always have them reviewed and notarized per your state’s requirements.

item.title: README
item.description: This folder contains legal templates for estate planning and incapacity planning for [Mother’s Name].To finalize: Fill in all names, dates, and addresses. Print and have signed before two witnesses and a notary. Store one notarized copy at home, one with your attorney, and one with your healthcare agent. ⚠️ Always verify your state’s witnessing and notarization laws.

item.title: Final Wishes Addendum
item.description: I wish to be: Buried Cremated Donated for medical research Preferred Funeral Home: [Name] Desired Location/Plot: [Description]Preferred songs, readings, or themes: “__” To my loved ones: “__” My digital accounts, passwords, and photos are stored at: [Location / Cloud Vault / Drive folder]Signature: ___ Date: ___

item.title: Beneficiary Designations
item.description: This document lists designated beneficiaries for accounts that pass outside the will.Reviewed and Updated: [Date]

item.title: Revocable Living Trust Agreement
item.description: This Revocable Living Trust is made on [Date] by [Mother’s Full Legal Name], as Grantor and Trustee.Grantor transfers into the Trust all property listed in Schedule A.Upon the death of the Grantor, all trust assets shall be distributed to Cody Rice-Velasquez.If the Grantor is unable to act, Cody Rice-Velasquez shall serve as Successor Trustee with full powers to administer, manage, and distribute assets.This trust may be revoked or amended by the Grantor at any time during her lifetime.Signature of Grantor: ___ Date: ___Witnesses: ___ Notary: ___

item.title: HIPAA Authorization
item.description: I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], authorize the release of my protected health information (PHI) to the following:Primary Authorized Recipient: Cody Rice-Velasquez Address: [Address] Relationship: Son / Health Care ProxyI understand this authorization allows my providers to: Disclose medical history, treatment, and billing information. Discuss my condition and care with the named individual(s). This authorization remains valid until revoked in writing or until my death.Signature: ___ Date: ___Witness: ___

item.title: Durable Power of Attorney for Finances
item.description: I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], designate Cody Rice-Velasquez as my Attorney-in-Fact, with full authority to handle all financial and legal matters on my behalf.My Agent shall have authority to: Access and manage bank accounts, investments, and retirement funds. Pay bills, file taxes, and manage property. Buy, sell, lease, or transfer property. Handle insurance, benefits, and claims. Engage attorneys, accountants, or financial advisors as needed. This Power of Attorney is durable, remaining effective even if I become incapacitated.Effective Date: Immediately upon signing. Governing Law: State of [Your State].Signature: ___ Date: ___Witnesses: Notary: ___

item.title: Living Will and Power of Attorney for Health Care and Property
item.description: I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], being of sound mind and over the age of eighteen (18), make this declaration to direct my family, physician, and all concerned as to my wishes for medical care, end-of-life decisions, and distribution of my property in the event I become unable to communicate my decisions.Address: [Full Address] Date of Birth: [MM/DD/YYYY]I hereby designate [Full Legal Name of Son/Daughter: Cody Rice-Velasquez], of [Address, City, State, Zip], as my Primary Health Care Agent and Durable Power of Attorney for Health Care.If my Primary Agent is unable or unwilling to serve, I designate: Alternate Agent: [Full Legal Name] Address: [Address] Relationship: [Relationship to Declarant] My Agent shall have full authority to make all health care decisions on my behalf, including but not limited to: Consent to or refusal of any medical treatment, procedure, medication, or surgery. Access to my medical records. Admission to or discharge from hospitals, nursing homes, or hospice care. Authorization of organ donation or autopsy. End-of-life decisions, including withdrawal of life-support, resuscitation (DNR/DNI), and comfort care. This Power of Attorney becomes effective immediately and remains valid even if I become incapacitated.If I have an incurable or irreversible condition and my attending physician has determined that my death is imminent, or if I am in a persistent vegetative state with no reasonable expectation of recovery, I direct that: Life-sustaining treatment (including CPR, mechanical ventilation, or tube feeding) shall not be administered if it only prolongs the dying process. I wish to receive comfort care and pain management even if it may hasten death. I do / do not wish to donate my organs and tissues. (Circle one and specify wishes here: ____) I appoint [Cody Rice-Velasquez] as my Attorney-in-Fact for all financial and property matters.This includes the authority to: Access and manage all bank accounts, investments, and property. Pay bills, taxes, and debts. Buy, sell, or transfer property in my name. Apply for benefits (Social Security, Medicare, etc.). File and manage legal or tax documents. This authority shall be durable and remain in effect in the event of my incapacity.Upon my death, I direct that all my personal and real property, including but not limited to household goods, vehicles, jewelry, and financial accounts, shall pass to:Primary Beneficiary: [Cody Rice-Velasquez], my child.If the above beneficiary does not survive me, I designate: Alternate Beneficiary: [Name, Relationship, Address]If guardianship or conservatorship becomes necessary, I nominate [Cody Rice-Velasquez] to serve in that role.Declarant Signature: ___ Name: [Full Legal Name of Mother] Date: [Date]Witness #1: Signature: ___ Printed Name: ____ Address: _ Date: ____Witness #2: Signature: ___ Printed Name: ____ Address: _ Date: ____STATE OF __ COUNTY OF ____Subscribed and sworn before me on this _ day of ____, 20____, by [Full Legal Name of Mother], who is personally known to me or has produced valid identification.Notary Public: ___ My Commission Expires: __ Seal: ☐
item.dc:creator: [Full Legal Name of Mother]

item.title: Last Will and Testament
item.description: I, [Full Legal Name of Mother], of [City, State], being of sound mind, declare this to be my Last Will and Testament.I revoke all prior wills and codicils.I appoint [Cody Rice-Velasquez] as my Executor (Personal Representative).If he cannot serve, I appoint [Alternate Representative].I give, devise, and bequeath all my tangible and intangible property to Cody Rice-Velasquez, my child, including all real estate, vehicles, jewelry, bank accounts, and personal effects.If he does not survive me, I designate [Alternate Beneficiary].If any minor dependents exist, I nominate Cody Rice-Velasquez as guardian of their person and estate.All debts and taxes shall be paid from the estate before distribution.Signature of Testator: ___ Date: ___Witness #1: ___ Witness #2: ___Notary Public (if required): ___
